dialogueChunks.intro: The doors opened this morning. Twenty-eight walked out. Twelve didn't. My sister was one of the twenty-eight.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The partial cure worked better than {target_npc} expected. Not everyone could be saved, and the families of those who didn't make it aren't ready to call this a victory. But the council never had to seal the doors. {quest_ally} is already distributing the remaining remedy to the outer settlements.
dialogueChunks.outro: You gave us a choice that wasn't between terrible and worse, {player_name}. Twelve graves is a tragedy. Forty would have been an atrocity. I'll carry the weight of the ones we lost, but I'll carry it standing up.

playerResponses.text: Mourn the twelve. But don't forget the twenty-eight.
I wish I could have saved them all.
Make sure the council never faces that vote again.

dialogueChunks.intro: Twelve hostages. All twelve. Walking through the gates right now. Some of them are injured, but they're alive.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {enemy_type} didn't expect a strike on both camps simultaneously. {target_npc} coordinated the timing while {quest_ally} led the second team. It was close: closer than anyone wants to admit. But nobody was left behind. The grain reserves are untouched. The hostage-takers are scattered.
dialogueChunks.outro: You found the third option, {player_name}. The one nobody believed existed. Twelve families are whole tonight because you refused to accept the impossible choice.

playerResponses.text: There's always a third option. You just have to be stubborn enough to find it.
The raiders will try again. Be ready next time.

dialogueChunks.intro: The bridge is gone. It collapsed an hour after the last person crossed. The commander never had to light the torch.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: All seventeen made it across the ford. The elder had to be carried the last mile, and one of the children fell asleep on a soldier's back. {target_npc} said the sound of the bridge falling behind them was the worst and best thing they ever heard. The {enemy_type} are on the far side now with no way across.
dialogueChunks.outro: You found the path nobody was looking for, {player_name}. Seventeen people are alive because you wouldn't accept that saving the many meant abandoning the few. The commander wants to shake your hand. I think he's also a little relieved he didn't have to make the call.

playerResponses.text: Those children sleeping safe tonight is all the thanks I need.
Rebuild the defenses. They'll find another way across eventually.
